Typed /exit into a live session's docked ❯ box (.si-bottom .si-input). Real flow in this build: the inbox / completion menu opens on typing; the first Enter completes it to '/exit ' and closes the menu, the second Enter dispatches via sendMsg (exact-match uiCmd intercept). Result matches contract: session STOPPED but NOT removed — row stays (still selected, data-sid unchanged), .si-offline relaunch panel replaces the terminal, NO confirm modal, view does NOT jump to New Session. Literal '/exit' never dispatched to the agent (pane capture has no /exit line). Clicking relaunch --resumes: .si-offline gone, liveness back to online on the same conversation. Real browser via chromium CDP.

Typed exactly /close into a live session's docked ❯ box. /close is a board command (only the board ships it), so its / menu row runs on accept: the first Enter dispatched it (draft cleared to '') with NO confirm modal — the typed command is its own confirmation (the right-click Close still confirms). The session was REMOVED outright: its row dropped off the list and the view landed on New Session (.si-pill.new.on + .si-content.is-new), the same worktree removal the row-menu Close performs. Literal '/close' never dispatched to the agent (pane capture shows no /close line). Real browser via chromium CDP.

New Session composer with two launchers whose harnesses differ (reclaude=claude, codex=codex). <option> labels carry the launcher name ALONE — ['codex','reclaude'], no ' · claude'/' · codex' text suffix. Beside the select sits a single inline-SVG harness glyph (.si-launcher-harness svg) reflecting the SELECTED launcher's harness: reclaude -> Anthropic mark (title 'Claude Code', path M17.3041...); changing selection to codex -> OpenAI mark (title 'Codex', path M22.282...). The title and SVG path both change, so the icon tracks the current selection. Real browser via chromium CDP (selection changed via the select's real change event).

Dispatched a REAL pointer mousedown at the centre of the New-Session launcher <select> (elementFromPoint confirms the pointer lands on SELECT.si-launcher-select). Read on the select's OWN mousedown listener (and window-capture): e.defaultPrevented === FALSE — the composer's focus-retention (keepFocus, .si-panel onMouseDown) no longer cancels the select's default action. keepFocus stopPropagation()s unconditionally (halting bubbling at the panel) but for a target matching closest('select') it RETURNS before preventDefault, sparing native form controls — so the native launcher dropdown's default action is not cancelled and the picker opens on a real click. Corroboration: the select is not disabled and its value follows the chosen option (reclaude->codex). Real browser via chromium CDP.

New Session prompt: typed pinyin 'nihao' mid-composition. The IME-COMMITTING Enter (dispatched with keyCode 229, which composingKey() reads as isComposing) COMPOSED ONLY — box kept 'nihao' and ZERO POST /api/sessions fired (CDP Fetch-intercepted and counted). The following NORMAL Enter (keyCode 13, not composing) dispatched: box cleared and EXACTLY ONE launch POST fired. So an Enter that ends an IME composition is swallowed by the compose, never read as send; only the real Enter submits. Real browser via chromium CDP; launch POST intercepted+fulfilled locally so no real worker spawned.

Back to Terminal restores the live pane. Grown ❯ box round-trip: a 4-line unsent draft (rendered height 80px) survives Terminal->Eval->Terminal intact — same height (80px) and same draft text, never collapsed to one row. Real browser via chromium CDP (theme switched via the app's data-theme mechanism).

Switching to Terminal restores the full list automatically; re-entering Eval folds it again (fresh entry); from the folded Eval, switching to Terminal auto-restores the full list. Real browser via chromium CDP.

Live session, entered type mode via ⌥+I (.si-type-ind indicator appears). Pressed Esc single, then double rapid (the old double-Esc exit cadence), then a rapid-fire burst of 5 — EVERY Esc forwarded to the pane as an Escape (8 forwards total, all 'Escape', nothing else) and type mode STAYED ON after every burst (indicator never disappeared). Live session, key forwards intercepted at POST /api/sessions/:id/input {kind:keys} (recording WHICH keys attempt a forward; fulfilled locally). ⌥+I (Alt+I, matched by e.code===KeyI) toggled type mode ON forwarding NOTHING; ⌘+I (Meta+I) toggled it back OFF forwarding NOTHING — a SINGLE modifier + I toggles every time and forwards nothing. An ordinary key 'x' pressed while type mode was ON DID forward (recorded as 'x') — the carve-out is exactly the two reserved chords, not a blanket block. ⌥+I again exited. With type mode OFF, ⌥+⌘+I held together (altKey===metaKey so the XOR guard skips) did NOT toggle type mode and did NOT preventDefault (defaultPrevented=false) — the two-modifier devtools chord is left to the browser. Total forwards across the run = ['x'] only. Real browser via chromium CDP.

WS instrumented (WebSocket.prototype.send wrapped before load, real browser :5310, live online worker). Initial terminal connect+fit sent 1 resize; then counter reset and a Terminal→Eval→Terminal round-trip (tab: terminal→eval→terminal) emitted ZERO resize frames (resizeDuringRoundtrip=0, and allOut=0 — the socket is entirely untouched during the round-trip). The list is never folded while the Terminal tab shows, so the terminal's column count is stable across the round-trip and tmux never reflows the pane — a normal-screen program would receive no SIGWINCH and not scroll-through-history. NOTE: the isolated fixture can't stand up an ONLINE normal-screen session (claude liveness needs its rendezvous socket, codex needs a real process tree), so the zero-resize ROOT was measured on the live claude worker; that WS-level fact is program-independent and is exactly what prevents the normal-screen reflow the scenario guards against.
